4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

I'll start by analyzing the two fixes needed:

**Fix 1**: `Button.variant="link"` - The variant type changed to add `'stateful'`, but `'link'` is still a valid value in the new type. No code change needed since `"link"` is still valid.

**Fix 2**: `ChartDonutProps` was removed from `@patternfly/react-charts`, but the replacement is also `ChartDonutProps` with the same props. The import is `{ ChartDonut, ChartLabel }` - not importing `ChartDonutProps` directly. Need to check if `ChartDonutProps` is used as a type anywhere in the file.

Let me read the file to understand the full context:

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1**: `Button.variant="link"` on lines 65 and 77. The type changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`. The value `"link"` is still valid in both old and new types — a new value `'stateful'` was added, but no existing values were removed. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2**: `ChartDonutProps` was removed from `@patternfly/react-charts`. Looking at the file, line 13 imports `{ ChartDonut, ChartLabel }` — it imports the *component* `ChartDonut`, not the *type* `ChartDonutProps`. The component `ChartDonut` is used on line 88, and all the props used (`ariaDesc`, `ariaTitle`, `height`, `width`, `radius`, `data`, `labels`, `subTitle`, `title`, `constrainToVisibleArea`, `subTitleComponent`) are all listed in the member mappings as unchanged. The fix description says "migrate to `ChartDonutProps`" (same name), and all props map 1:1. Since this file doesn't reference `ChartDonutProps` directly and the component + its props haven't changed, **no code change needed.**

Both fixes indicate that the existing code is already compatible with the new API. The file requires no modifications.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (`Button.variant` type changed): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The value `"link"` used on lines 65 and 77 remains valid in the new type. The only change to the type was the addition of the new `'stateful'` variant, which doesn't affect existing usage.

2. **Fix 2** (`ChartDonutProps` removed): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The file imports the `ChartDonut` component (not `ChartDonutProps` type), and all props used on the `<ChartDonut>` component (`ariaDesc`, `ariaTitle`, `height`, `width`, `radius`, `data`, `labels`, `subTitle`, `title`, `constrainToVisibleArea`, `subTitleComponent`) map 1:1 to the replacement interface with unchanged names.

Code context:
```
   12  import * as _ from 'lodash-es';
   13  import { ChartDonut, ChartLabel } from '@patternfly/react-charts';
   14  import { Button } from '@patternfly/react-core';

```

### Fix 2
Lines: 64, 76
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: div

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 64):
   63        <div className="odf-install__stats">
   64          <Button
   65            variant="link"

  (line 76):
   75          <div className="odf-install_stats--divider" />
   76          <Button
   77            variant="link"

```

1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/create-storage-system/create-storage-system-steps/create-local-volume-set-step/selected-capacity.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the file to understand the current code.

Now let me analyze each fix:

### Fix 1: `ChartLabelProps` removed
The rule says `ChartLabelProps` was removed but the replacement is also called `ChartLabelProps` with all the same props. Looking at the actual code, `ChartLabel` is imported (not `ChartLabelProps`) and used on lines 104-107. The usage is:
```tsx
<ChartLabel dy={5} style={{ fill: `var(--pf-t--color--gray--40)` }} />
```
The props `dy` and `style` are both in the matching props list. The component `ChartLabel` itself is still available (the rule is about the *Props interface*, not the component). Since the file imports `ChartLabel` (the component), not `ChartLabelProps` (the interface), and all props used (`dy`, `style`) map 1:1, **no change is needed**.

### Fix 2: Icon children to `icon` prop on `Button`
Looking at lines 64-86, both `Button` components contain **text content** (translated strings), not icon elements. Neither Button has an icon from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The children are `{t('{{nodes, number}} Node', ...)}` and `{t('{{disks, number}} Disk', ...)}`. These are text-only buttons with `variant="link"`.

The rule says "icon content in `<Button>` must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children." But there are no icons here — the content is purely text. **No change is needed.**

Let me also check line 158 which has another Button:

Line 158: `<Button key="confirm" variant="primary" onClick={onCancel}>` with child `{t('Close')}` — also text-only, no icon.

All three Button instances in this file contain only text children, no icons. No changes are required for Fix 2.

The file is already correct as-is. No edits needed.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (`ChartLabelProps` removed): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The file imports `ChartLabel` (the component), not `ChartLabelProps` (the interface). The component is still available and the props used (`dy`, `style`) are all present in the replacement interface with identical names.

2. **Fix 2** (icon children to `icon` prop on `Button`): **Already compatible — no change needed.** All three `<Button>` instances in this file (lines 64, 76, and 158) contain only text children (translated strings via `t()`), not icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. The rule only applies when a `<Button>` contains icon elements as children, which is not the case here.

Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />
